Title: Coordinated bacterial and plant sulfur metabolism in Enterobacter sp. SA187–induced plant salt stress tolerance
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not primarily expression-profiled as a focal DEG; tested genetically under salt stress with SA187
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: PAP-associated sulfur signaling; downstream sulfur metabolites
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stress signaling and acclimation under salinity
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: fry1 mutants were hypersensitive to salt stress and significantly rescued by SA187, showing the PAP/retrograde sulfur-monitoring pathway contributes to SA187-induced tolerance
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping under salt stress with/without SA187
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FRY1 links sulfur metabolism to PAP retrograde signaling. 2. fry1 plants were salt hypersensitive. 3. SA187 significantly suppressed the phenotype, implicating this signaling branch in microbial stress protection.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study treats FRY1 as part of sulfur monitoring rather than as a biosynthetic enzyme or transcription factor.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for preserving sulfur-signaling competence in stress-resilient plants used with microbial inoculants

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: amiRNA transgene in mutant line; native genes in WT
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: LSU genes are sulfur-responsive; discussed as part of sulfur regulon
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Function tested under salt stress with SA187
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Reactive oxygen species detoxification capacity linked to sulfur metabolism; glutathione-associated redox state
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: amiR-LSUc plants were hypersensitive to salt stress and accumulated high ROS, both of which were strongly/completely suppressed by SA187
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: amiR mutant phenotyping; nitroblue tetrazolium ROS staining under salt stress with/without SA187
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LSU genes act as central regulators linking sulfur metabolism to chloroplast superoxide dismutase activity. 2. amiR-LSUc plants showed elevated ROS and salt hypersensitivity. 3. SA187 suppressed both ROS accumulation and growth defects, implicating the LSU pathway in the microbial benefit.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats LSU proteins as chloroplast-targeted hubs rather than enzymes or TFs; all four genes were jointly targeted in amiR-LSUc.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising targets for reinforcing chloroplast redox protection in combination with beneficial microbes under saline agriculture
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of LSU function increases ROS and salt sensitivity

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not directly profiled as focal DEG in this study
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Superoxide detoxification / ROS homeostasis associated with sulfur metabolism
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: FSD2 is identified as the chloroplast superoxide dismutase activated by LSU proteins in the pathway through which SA187 suppresses salt-induced ROS accumulation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Inference from prior interaction evidence integrated with ROS phenotyping of amiR-LSUc in this study
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FSD2 is the chloroplast ROS-detoxifying component linked to LSU proteins. 2. SA187 suppression of LSU mutant ROS phenotype implicates this axis in the beneficial interaction. 3. The study positions FSD2 downstream of sulfur/LSU regulation in salt tolerance.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FSD2 was not directly manipulated here, so evidence is indirect within this paper.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential complementary target for improving chloroplast ROS detoxification in stress-tolerant crops

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Up-regulated in endophytic SA187 upon interaction with Arabidopsis, under both salt and non-salt conditions as part of the sulfur pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports salt stress tolerance via sulfur assimilation and downstream ethylene/redox-related metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces adenosine 5'-phosphosulfate (APS) to sulfite in bacterial sulfate assimilation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Up-regulation of bacterial cysH supports sulfur assimilation in SA187 and contributes to metabolite provision associated with Arabidopsis salt tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dual RNA-seq; qRT-PCR validation of selected bacterial DEGs; plant salt-tolerance phenotyping with SA187 colonization
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Endophytic interaction with Arabidopsis; 100 mM NaCl salt stress
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established in SA187 in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cysH was part of the SA187 sulfur pathway induced during Arabidopsis colonization. 2. Its induction supports the conclusion that the entire bacterial sulfur assimilation pathway is activated in planta. 3. This coordinated sulfur metabolism is linked to host salt stress tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although cysH is highlighted in the conclusions by comparison to a different bacterium study, the present paper did not functionally manipulate SA187 cysH directly.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target in beneficial microbes to enhance sulfur-derived metabolite support for crop salt tolerance

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly up-regulated in endophytic SA187 during Arabidopsis interaction
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Sulfur-containing amino acid supporting methionine, glutathione, and stress-protective metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes synthesis of L-cysteine from O-acetylserine and sulfide
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induced cysK in SA187 supports increased sulfur assimilation and production of cysteine-related metabolites linked to plant salt tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dual RNA-seq; qRT-PCR validation of selected bacterial genes; Arabidopsis metabolite profiling for cystine/methionine; salt-tolerance phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: cysH; cysND; cysC; CBS; TCH; metE
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cysK was up-regulated in endophytic SA187. 2. This supports enhanced bacterial cysteine biosynthesis during colonization. 3. Elevated sulfur amino acid metabolism correlates with improved Arabidopsis salt tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Plant roots accumulated more cystine under SA187 colonization, but the paper does not prove direct transfer of cysteine from bacterium to plant.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate microbial engineering target to strengthen sulfur amino acid provisioning in bioinoculants

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly up-regulated in endophytic SA187 upon Arabidopsis interaction
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Methionine supports sulfur metabolism and serves as precursor of KMBA/ethylene-related salt tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Up-regulated bacterial metE supports methionine and downstream KMBA-associated production linked to Arabidopsis salt tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dual RNA-seq; qRT-PCR validation of selected bacterial DEGs; Arabidopsis root methionine quantification; exogenous methionine rescue assay
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CBS; TCH; MetR; TyrB; MtnN; MtnK; MtnC; MtnD; SpeD
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. metE was strongly up-regulated during SA187 colonization. 2. The induced methionine synthesis branch supports production of sulfur metabolites and the KMBA route. 3. Exogenous methionine mimicked the beneficial growth effect under salt stress.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study links metE induction to global sulfur pathway activation, not to direct genetic perturbation of metE.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful microbial target to enhance methionine/KMBA-related plant stress protection

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Name: MtnN; MtnK; MtnC; MtnD; TyrB; SpeD
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bacterial metabolite promoting Arabidopsis salt tolerance, likely via conversion to ethylene in planta
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Plant-associated context; direct organ accumulation not shown here
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Mixed enzyme families of methionine salvage and polyamine/SAM metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Participate in the methionine salvage pathway leading to KMBA-related sulfur metabolite production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Up-regulation of methionine salvage genes supports production of KMBA, a key SA187-derived metabolite that promotes plant salt tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dual RNA-seq; prior genetic/biochemical evidence from cited earlier work; KMBA treatment phenocopy in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Multiple methionine salvage genes were up-regulated in endophytic SA187. 2. This supports enhanced KMBA-related metabolism during colonization. 3. KMBA treatment recapitulated part of the beneficial salt-tolerance response in Arabidopsis.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: GSE124591; GSE102950; plant RNA-seq: GSE145884
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: These genes are grouped because the paper discusses them collectively and does not provide individual functional dissection in the Results.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important microbial engineering module for inoculants designed to improve crop salt tolerance through sulfur/ethylene-associated metabolite output

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Sulfate transporter transcripts were differentially regulated in shoots and roots under SA187 colonization and salt stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots/shoots under 100 mM NaCl with SA187; sulfur regulon-responsive
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports sulfur assimilation required for amino acid and antioxidant production during salt tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: sultr1;2 mutants were salt hypersensitive and this phenotype was strongly rescued by SA187, linking sulfate uptake to SA187-induced salt tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping under 100 mM NaCl with/without SA187; ICP-OES sulfur quantification; transcriptomics
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 100 mM NaCl; SA187 colonization; sulfate supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SULTR transporter genes were transcriptionally modulated by SA187. 2. The sultr1;2 mutant was hypersensitive to salt. 3. SA187 largely rescued the salt-sensitive phenotype, showing tight coupling between microbial benefit and host sulfate uptake.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Rescue likely reflects pathway redundancy and/or metabolite complementation rather than direct restoration of transporter function.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Host target for improving sulfur acquisition under salinity, potentially in combination with beneficial microbes
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutants showed growth defects also under normal conditions

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: APS kinase transcripts were differentially regulated by SA187 in roots and shoots under salt stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots and shoots under 100 mM NaCl with SA187/KMBA/ACC treatments
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to sulfur flux and sulfation-related metabolism during stress adaptation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Phosphorylates APS to form PAPS, donor for sulfotransfer reactions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: apk1 apk2 mutants were salt hypersensitive and significantly rescued by SA187, indicating involvement of the APS/PAPS branch in SA187-mediated tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. APK genes were part of the SA187-responsive sulfur regulon. 2. apk1 apk2 mutants showed salt hypersensitivity. 3. SA187 significantly rescued the phenotype, connecting the PAPS branch to microbial stress protection.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper assayed the double mutant rather than individual gene contributions.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate host pathway node for optimization of sulfur partitioning and stress resilience with microbial inoculants
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Growth defects under nonstress conditions were noted for sulfur-pathway mutants

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: APR transcripts were differentially regulated in roots and shoots by SA187, KMBA, and ACC under salt stress
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential sulfur assimilation products for antioxidant and stress-protective metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces APS to sulfite in plant sulfur assimilation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: apr2 mutants were salt hypersensitive but almost completely rescued by SA187, showing APR2 contributes to host sulfur assimilation during microbial protection
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established for APR2 specifically in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. APR genes belong to the sulfur regulon altered by SA187. 2. apr2 mutants were hypersensitive to salt. 3. SA187 substantially rescued the phenotype, likely owing to pathway redundancy and microbial compensation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The discussion explicitly notes redundancy of APR genes may explain strong rescue.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful host sulfur-assimilation target in salinity breeding, especially combined with beneficial microbes
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutants exhibited growth defects even without salt

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: SIR1 transcript was differentially regulated under SA187 colonization in roots/shoots under salt stress
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supplies reduced sulfur required for sulfur amino acids and redox buffering
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces sulfite to sulfide for subsequent cysteine synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: sir1 mutants were strongly salt hypersensitive and only weakly rescued by SA187, demonstrating that SIR1 is necessary for effective SA187-induced salt stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SIR1 is part of the SA187-responsive sulfur assimilation network. 2. sir1 mutants showed severe salt sensitivity. 3. SA187 could hardly rescue sir1, indicating SIR1 is a bottleneck essential for microbial stress protection.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The discussion explicitly describes SIR1 as a bottleneck limiting cysteine, methionine, and downstream products.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value host target; maintaining SIR1 function appears critical for exploiting microbial sulfur-based stress tolerance

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly up-regulated in endophytic SA187 during interaction with Arabidopsis
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports salt stress tolerance via sulfur metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates methionine synthesis branch genes during endophytic interaction
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: LysR-type or MetR-family bacterial transcriptional regulator
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induced MetR accompanies activation of methionine biosynthesis in SA187, supporting sulfur metabolite production linked to plant salt tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dual RNA-seq; qRT-PCR validation of selected genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller of bacterial methionine branch activation in planta
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MetR was up-regulated during Arabidopsis colonization by SA187. 2. This coincided with induction of methionine biosynthetic genes including metE. 3. The result supports regulatory activation of bacterial sulfur amino acid metabolism during the beneficial interaction.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct mutant or binding assay for MetR was performed in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential microbial engineering regulator for coordinated activation of sulfur amino acid metabolism in plant probiotics

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Differentially regulated in shoots and roots of salt-stressed plants colonized by SA187; also responsive to KMBA and ACC treatments
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots and shoots under 100 mM NaCl plus SA187/KMBA/ACC
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to salt stress tolerance through sulfur regulon control
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SULTR; ATPS; APK; APR; SIR1 and sulfur regulon genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Regulates sulfur assimilation genes in response to sulfur/ethylene-related signals
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Can form homo-/heterodimeric relationships within EIN3/EIL family as discussed
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Sulfur status; ethylene-related signaling; SA187-derived KMBA
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of sulfur status/ethylene feedback loop proposed in the model
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SA187 modulates SLIM1 expression as part of reprogramming the Arabidopsis sulfur regulon under salt stress
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Plant RNA-seq; qRT-PCR validation; comparative KMBA/ACC transcriptomics
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 100 mM NaCl; SA187 colonization; KMBA; ACC
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central transcriptional regulator of plant sulfur response and metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SLIM1 transcript levels were altered by SA187 under salt stress. 2. KMBA and ACC elicited related sulfur regulon responses. 3. The data support a model in which SA187 influences sulfur metabolism through ethylene-linked SLIM1 regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SLIM1 was not genetically tested in this study, but is central to the mechanistic model.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulatory node for tuning sulfur nutrition and stress resilience, though pleiotropy should be considered

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Differentially regulated in salt-stressed shoots and roots by SA187; also affected by KMBA and ACC
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports salt tolerance through regulation of sulfur-associated defense/redox metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Associated with modulation of sulfur metabolic gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: EIN3 family interaction network with SLIM1/EILs discussed
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of proposed sulfur-ethylene feedback loop
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SA187 and KMBA/ACC alter EIN3-associated signaling linked to sulfur regulon reprogramming under salt stress
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq comparison among SA187, KMBA, and ACC treatments
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 100 mM NaCl; SA187 colonization; KMBA; ACC
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller linking ethylene signaling to sulfur metabolic output
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EIN3 expression was altered by SA187 in salt-stressed Arabidopsis. 2. Similar responses to KMBA and ACC support involvement of ethylene signaling. 3. EIN3 is incorporated into the model connecting SA187-derived metabolites to sulfur metabolism and tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional causality is model-supported rather than mutant-tested here.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulator for integrating ethylene and sulfur responses in stress-tolerance engineering, with caution for pleiotropic ethylene effects

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Differentially regulated in salt-stressed tissues under SA187 treatment
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots and shoots under 100 mM NaCl plus SA187
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to salt stress adaptation through sulfur response regulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Associated with sulfur regulon modulation under SA187 colonization
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Possible homo-/heterodimeric interactions within EIN3/EIL family discussed
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Proposed component of feedback between sulfur status and ethylene signaling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: EIL2 is among sulfur-related transcription factors reprogrammed by SA187 under salt stress
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent transcriptional controller of sulfur-response network
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EIL2 expression was altered in SA187-colonized plants under salt stress. 2. It belongs to the sulfur/ethylene signaling module highlighted by the study. 3. The data support coordinated transcriptional regulation of host sulfur metabolism by SA187.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct genetic test of EIL2 in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential supportive regulator in sulfur/ethylene network engineering
